H2802.derivation: from H2801 (חָרַת) (but equivalent to H2793 (חֹרֶשׁ)); forest;

H2803.strongs_def: properly, to plait or interpenetrate, i.e. (literally) to weave or (generally) to fabricate; figuratively, to plot or contrive (usually in a malicious sense); hence (from the mental effort) to think, regard, value, compute
H2803.kjv_def: (make) account (of), conceive, consider, count, cunning (man, work, workman), devise, esteem, find out, forecast, hold, imagine, impute, invent, be like, mean, purpose, reckon(-ing be made), regard, think.
H2803.usage: count(23x), devise(22x), think(18x), imagine(9x), cunning(8x), reckon(7x), purpose(6x), esteem(6x), account(5x), impute(4x), forecast(2x), regard(2x), workman(2x), conceived(1x)

H2805.strongs_def: a belt or strap (as being interlaced)

H2806.derivation: from H2803 (חָשַׁב) and H1777 (דִּין); considerate judge;

H2809.strongs_def: Cheshbon, a place East of the Jordan

H2810.strongs_def: a contrivance, i.e. actual (a warlike machine) or mental (a machination)

H2811.derivation: or חֲשַׁבְיָהוּ; from H2803 (חָשַׁב) and H3050 (יָהּ); Jah has regarded;
H2811.strongs_def: Chashabjah, the name of nine Israelites

H2813.derivation: from H2808 (חֶשְׁבּוֹן) and H3050 (יָהּ); thought of Jah;
H2813.strongs_def: Chashabnejah, the name of two Israelites

H2814.kjv_def: hold peace, keep silence, be silent, (be) still.

H2815.strongs_def: Chashshub, the name of two or three Israelites

H2816.derivation: (Aramaic) from a root corresponding to H2821 (חָשַׁךְ);

H2818.derivation: (Aramaic) a collateral root to one corresponding to H2363 (חוּשׁ) in the sense of readiness;
H2818.strongs_def: to be necessary (from the idea of convenience) or (transitively) to need

H2819.derivation: from a root corresponding to H2818 (חֲשַׁח);

H2820.derivation: a primitive root; also interchangeable  with H2821 (חָשַׁךְ)
H2820.strongs_def: to restrain or (reflex.) refrain; by implication, to refuse, spare, preserve; to observe
H2820.kjv_def: assuage, [idiom] darken, forbear, hinder, hold back, keep (back), punish, refrain, reserve, spare, withhold.
H2820.usage: spare(8x), withhold(3x), refrain(3x), asswage(2x), reserved(2x), variant(1x), forbear(1x), hindereth(1x), kept(1x), punished(1x), withholdeth(1x)

H2821.strongs_def: to be dark (as withholding light); transitively, to darken
H2821.kjv_def: be black, be (make) dark, darken, cause darkness, be dim, hide.
H2821.usage: darken(9x), dark(5x), blacker(1x), darkness(1x), dim(1x), hideth(1x), variant(1x)

H2822.strongs_def: the dark; hence (literally) darkness; figuratively, misery, destruction, death, ignorance, sorrow, wickedness

H2825.derivation: or חֲשֵׁיכָהxlit chăshêkâh corrected to chăshêykâh; from H2821 (חָשַׁךְ);

H2826.strongs_def: to make (intrans. be) unsteady, i.e. weak

H2828.derivation: from the same as H2831 (חַשְׁמַן); enriched;
H2828.strongs_def: Chashum, the name of two or three Israelites

H2830.strongs_def: probably bronze or polished spectrum metal

H2831.derivation: from an unused root (probably meaning firm or capacious in resources); apparently

H2832.strongs_def: Chasmonah, a place in the Desert

H2833.derivation: from an unused root probably meaning to contain or sparkle;
H2833.strongs_def: perhaps a pocket (as holding the Urim and Thummim), or rich (as containing gems), used only of the gorget of the highpriest

H2834.strongs_def: to strip off, i.e. generally to make naked (for exertion or in disgrace), to drain away or bail up (a liquid)
H2834.kjv_def: make bare, clean, discover, draw out, take, uncover.

H2835.strongs_def: properly, drawn off, i.e. separated; hence, a small company (as divided from the rest)

H2836.derivation: a primitive root; also interchangeable for H2820 (חָשַׂךְ)
H2836.strongs_def: to cling, i.e. join, (figuratively) to love, delight in; elliptically; to deliver
H2836.kjv_def: have a delight, (have a) desire, fillet, long, set (in) love.

H2838.derivation: or חָשׁוּק; past participle of H2836 (חָשַׁק);
H2838.strongs_def: attached, i.e. a fence-rail or rod connecting the posts or pillars

H2839.strongs_def: conjoined, i.e. a wheel-spoke or rod connecting the hub with the rim

H2840.derivation: from an unused root meaning to bind together;
H2840.strongs_def: combined, i.e. the nave or hub of awheel (as holding the spokes together)

H2841.strongs_def: properly, a combination or gathering, i.e. of watery clouds

H2843.strongs_def: a Chushathite or descendant of Chushah

H2844.strongs_def: concretely, crushed; also afraid; abstractly, terror

H2846.strongs_def: to lay hold of; especially to pick up fire

H2850.strongs_def: a Chittite, or descendant of Cheth

H2852.strongs_def: properly, to cut off, i.e. (figuratively) to decree

H2856.strongs_def: to close up; especially to seal
H2856.kjv_def: make an end, mark, seal (up), stop.

H2859.strongs_def: to give (a daughter) away in marriage; hence (generally) to contract affinity by marriage
H2859.kjv_def: join in affinity, father in law, make marriages, mother in law, son in law.

H2860.strongs_def: a relative by marriage (especially through the bride); figuratively, a circumcised child (as a species of religious espousal)

H2864.strongs_def: to force a passage, as by burglary; figuratively, with oars

H2865.strongs_def: properly, to prostrate; hence, to break down, either (literally) by violence, or (figuratively) by confusion and fear
H2865.kjv_def: abolish, affright, be (make) afraid, amaze, beat down, discourage, (cause to) dismay, go down, scare, terrify.
H2865.usage: dismayed(27x), afraid(6x), broken(3x), abolished(1x), affrighted(1x), amazed(1x), chapt(1x), confound(1x), discouraged(1x), scarest(1x), terrify(1x)

H2870.derivation: from H2895 (טוֹב) and H410 (אֵל); pleasing (to) God;
H2870.strongs_def: Tabeel, the name of a Syrian and of a Persian

H2871.strongs_def: properly, dyed, i.e. a turban (probably as of colored stuff)

H2872.derivation: from an unused root meaning to pile up;
H2872.strongs_def: properly, accumulated; i.e. (by implication) a summit

H2874.strongs_def: properly, something slaughtered; hence, a beast (or meat, as butchered); abstractly butchery (or concretely, a place of slaughter)

H2875.strongs_def: Tebach, the name of a Mesopotamian and of an Israelite

H2876.strongs_def: properly, a butcher; hence, a lifeguardsman (because he was acting as an executioner); also a cook (usually slaughtering the animal for food)

H2878.derivation: feminine of H2874 (טֶבַח) and meaning the same
H2878.strongs_def: {properly, something slaughtered; hence, a beast (or meat, as butchered); abstractly butchery (or concretely, a place of slaughter)}

H2882.derivation: from H2881 (טָבַל) and H3050 (יָהּ); Jah has dipped;

H2885.strongs_def: properly, a seal (as sunk into the wax), i.e. signet (for sealing); hence (generally) a ring of any kind

H2886.derivation: from H2895 (טוֹב) and H7417 (רִמּוֹן); pleasing (to) Rimmon;

H2888.strongs_def: Tabbath, a place East of the Jordan

H2889.strongs_def: pure (in a physical, chemical, ceremonial or moral sense)

H2891.derivation: a primitive root; properly, to be bright; i.e. (by implication)
H2891.strongs_def: to be pure (physical sound, clear, unadulterated; Levitically, uncontaminated; morally, innocent or holy)
H2891.kjv_def: be (make, make self, pronounce) clean, cleanse (self), purge, purify(-ier, self).

H2895.strongs_def: to be (transitively, do or make) good (or well) in the widest sense
H2895.kjv_def: be (do) better, cheer, be (do, seem) good, (make) goodly, [idiom] please, (be, do, go, play) well.
H2895.usage: good(9x), please(6x), goodly(2x), better(2x), cheer(1x), comely(1x), do(1x)

H2896.strongs_def: good (as an adjective) in the widest sense; used likewise as a noun, both in the masculine and the feminine, the singular and the plural (good, a good or good thing, a good man or woman; the good, goods or good things, good men or women), also as an adverb (well)
H2896.kjv_def: beautiful, best, better, bountiful, cheerful, at ease, [idiom] fair (word), (be in) favour, fine, glad, good (deed, -lier, -liest, -ly, -ness, -s), graciously, joyful, kindly, kindness, liketh (best), loving, merry, [idiom] most, pleasant, [phrase] pleaseth, pleasure, precious, prosperity, ready, sweet, wealth, welfare, (be) well(-favoured).
H2896.usage: good(361x), better(72x), well(20x), goodness(16x), goodly(9x), best(8x), merry(7x), fair(7x), prosperity(6x), precious(4x), fine(3x), wealth(3x), beautiful(2x), fairer(2x), favour(2x), glad(2x)

H2897.strongs_def: Tob, a region apparently East of the Jordan

H2898.strongs_def: good (as a noun), in the widest sense, especially goodness (superlative concretely, the best), beauty, gladness, welfare
H2898.kjv_def: fair, gladness, good(-ness, thing, -s), joy, go well with.
H2898.usage: goodness(14x), good(9x), goods(3x), fair(1x), gladness(1x), joy(1x), well(1x)

H2899.derivation: from H2896 (טוֹב) and H138 (אֲדֹנִיָּה); pleasing (to) Adonijah;
